https://uk.news.yahoo.com/turn-off-googl.title: ‘Turn It Off’—Google Update Starts Scanning Your Gmail
https://uk.news.yahoo.com/turn-off-googl.text:
URL Source: https://uk.news.yahoo.com/turn-off-google-starts-scanning-224243862.html

Published Time: 2026-05-04T22:42:43.000Z

Markdown Content:
"If you have a Gmail account," Shark Tank’s Lori Greiner posted on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DX4XsXyScKs/?igsh=MTc3MjgwZzh3M3c5OQ==) this weekend, "do not ignore this warning." The star claims “Google doesn’t want you to know this, but they’ve been allowing AI to scan every single one of your emails.”

This is the [AI upgrade](https://tech.yahoo.com/ai/gemini/articles/google-changes-gmail-gemini-attack-084831112.html) that I have warned Gmail users about before. By enabling its new smart features, Gemini crawls through your inbox to deliver smart searches, contextual replies and email summaries. It’s clearly a major change.

[MORE FROM FORBES‘Pay The Fine Now’—Feds Issue Text Warning For iPhone And Android Users By Zak Doffman](https://tech.yahoo.com/cybersecurity/articles/pay-fine-now-feds-issue-073158698.html)

“Here’s the good news," Greiner says, "you can turn it off in 30 seconds." Users are told to disable the two settings that switch all this on. You’ll find those in Smart Features and Personalisation settings, where you can control it for Gmail and other products. But you can’t dip in and out. if you disable it, you lose new AI upgrades.

Google asks me to stress that all these features are optional and users always remain in control. They’re likely enabled by default, so you do need to act if you want to disable them. Google is more concrete on data training, assuring that [user email content is never used to train its AI models](https://tech.yahoo.com/ai/gemini/articles/google-gmail-upgrade-decision-2-203737399.html) through this upgrade.

![Image 2: Gmail App](https://s.yimg.com/ny/api/res/1.2/Ux413wufAndEDXMHjUDprw--/YXBwaWQ9aGlnaGxhbmRlcjt3PTk2MDtoPTYzOTtjZj13ZWJw/https://media.zenfs.com/en/forbes_contributor_845/2df4430ab4927edd0a1691379a297b54)

Another Gmail AI warning.

dpa/picture alliance via Getty Images

While the viral post is new, the Gmail upgrade is not. What _is_ new is the enhanced [Personal Intelligence](https://tech.yahoo.com/ai/gemini/articles/google-starts-scanning-photos-goes-051819344.html) update that scans content across various Google platforms to storyboard your life, letting you [opt-in to connecting Google apps to Gemini](https://www.forbes.com/sites/zakdoffman/2026/01/14/google-confirms-new-upgrade-choice-for-millions-of-gmail-users/). You should make a decision on this as well — it’s more intrusive than just email.

The Gmail update isn’t as binary as Greiner suggests. Most users will take the convenience and accept the privacy risks. While Google is not a privacy-first brand, it’s trusted to secure sensitive user information — from everyone but itself.

[MORE FROM FORBES Google’s Android Update—iPhone Wins As Samsung Falls Behind By Zak Doffman](https://tech.yahoo.com/phones/articles/google-android-iphone-wins-samsung-060439133.html)

https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/boy.title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence
https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/boy.text:
URL Source: https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/boy-14-shot-dead-israeli-settlers-west-bank-escalation-violence-rcna342543

Published Time: 2026-05-02T11:00:41.000Z

Markdown Content:
May 2, 2026, 11:00 AM UTC

By Molly Hunter

AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead.

Aws al-Nasaan, 14, was gunned down in broad daylight last week in the small Palestinian village of Al-Mughayyir, in the occupied West Bank.

His death comes amid a dramatic escalation in settler violence, alongside a push to expand Israel's control over the West Bank through [new laws](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/israel-west-bank-land-registration-regulations-palestinians-annexation-rcna259222), [settlement expansions](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/israel-announces-west-bank-settlement-imperil-palestinian-state-rcna224944) and [security crackdowns](https://www.nbcnews.com/world/israel/netanyahu-convenes-cabinet-settler-violence-west-bank-continues-unabat-rcna245270) limiting Palestinians' freedom. According to [data](https://www.ochaopt.org/data/casualties) from the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, Israeli settlers and forces have killed at least 42 Palestinians so far this year.

The attack, caught on video from multiple angles, took place just after noon on Tuesday, April 21. Video obtained by NBC News shows a bearded man in an Israeli military uniform firing down the hill toward the school. He crouches to get a better angle and keeps shooting.

Video shot from the road to the east side of the school shows bullets hitting the wall. In one of the videos, al-Nasaan’s body can be seen on the ground as his friends shout for help.

![Image 1: Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor. ](https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-760w,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-05/260501-west-bank-teen-mb-1322-22d2d5.jpg)

Aws al-Nasaan is carried after being shot in the West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir. A pool of blood lies on the floor. Obtained by NBC News

“They ran out of the gates trying to escape,” the school’s principal Bassem Abu Assaf said, pointing out the bullet holes still visible in the stone wall last week. “The shooting was crazy, just crazy, nonstop shooting.”

Construction worker Jihad Abu Naim, 32, was also killed in the attack, the principal said.

“This is supposed to be a safe place,” said Abu Assaf, who is responsible for 460 students. “School is a right for every child,” he added. But, fearful of another attack, classes were cancelled for a week.

Palestinian eyewitnesses say Israeli soldiers were on the scene within minutes and did nothing to stop or apprehend the shooter. Instead, they lobbed tear gas into the crowd of students and villagers outside of the school, Abu Assaf and multiple eyewitnesses said.

https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/02/beyond.title: Beyond Lovable and Mistral: 21 European startups to watch
https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/02/beyond.text:
URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/02/beyond-lovable-and-mistral-21-european-startups-to-watch/

Published Time: 2026-05-02T14:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
Europe should be known for BottleCap AI, not [bottle cap memes](https://www.reddit.com/r/ExplainTheJoke/comments/1gllkyw/not_european_whats_up_with_the_bottle/). With its tongue-in-cheek name, this Prague-based AI startup is one of the teams that VCs think you should know.

It is not that European startups never cut through the noise — [Lovable](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/28/lovable-launches-its-vibe-coding-app-on-ios-and-android/) and [Mistral AI](https://techcrunch.com/2025/09/09/what-is-mistral-ai-everything-to-know-about-the-openai-competitor/) are proof of it. But there are many more that don’t have [nine digits in annual recurring revenue](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/lovable-says-it-added-100m-in-revenue-last-month-alone-with-just-146-employees/) yet and that insiders are still tracking very closely.

That’s where this list comes in. Over the last few weeks, we asked investors at some of Europe’s best known venture funds to recommend two startups each: one from their portfolio (because they liked the startup well enough to invest) and one outside of it (because they are the startup experts but can’t invest in them all). We also threw in a few picks of our own.

From pre-launch to [unicorn](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/31/meet-the-new-european-unicorns-of-2026/), these startups are at different stages in their journey, and from different sectors. Due to our methodology, they may not reflect where the region’s hottest hubs are, but they do reflect how [deep tech talent](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/30/76-european-deep-tech-university-spinouts-reached-unicorn-or-centaur-status/) could help Europe play its own cards in the AI race.

## Alta Ares

**_Recommended by Julien Codorniou, general partner, 20VC_.**

**What it does:**[Alta Ares](https://www.altaares.com/) develops AI-powered counter-drone systems.

**Why it’s worth watching:**Defense tech has gone from pariah to trending, particularly in Europe, where the war in Ukraine was a wake-up call for armies to modernize. Alta Ares’ interceptors answer a need for cheaper solutions to detect and fight drone incursions.

Techcrunch event

San Francisco, CA|October 13-15, 2026

## Apron

**_Recommended by Jan Hammer, partner, Index Ventures (investor)_.**

**What it does:**[Apron](https://getapron.com/) provides [invoice management for small business owners](https://techcrunch.com/2023/09/28/apron-grabs-15-million-to-simplify-invoice-processing/).

**Why it’s worth watching:**SMBs can be a lucrative segment for fintech companies; business owners are willing to spend at least some money to save time, and there are millions of them.

## Botify

**_Recommended by Claire Houry, general partner, Ventech (investor)_.**

**What it does:**[Botify](https://www.botify.com/) helps brands increase their visibility in AI searches.
